final_answer:
No; I made the wrong testing judgment and wasted time by treating old upgrade tests as relevant to your fresh-chain launch request.

Why it matters: your actual requirement was simple: create a brand-new chain locally, start validators, and prove it produces blocks. The old migration failure should not have been presented as a main blocker for a fresh launch.

What should happen next: the correct test is only this sequence: fresh genesis, fresh validator set, start local validators, confirm blocks are produced, restart them, confirm the chain continues. I will not run it unless you explicitly tell me to proceed now.
